evals.name: happy-path-architecture-design
evals.prompt: Design the software architecture for our order management system. It's a Java Spring Boot monolith that needs to support 1000 concurrent users.
evals.expected_output: Complete architecture documentation with module view, component view, design patterns, quality attribute scenarios, ADRs, and debt/evolution plan
expectations: Produces module view with decomposition logic and dependency graph
Includes component cards with interfaces and responsibilities
Selects and justifies design patterns against quality attributes
Defines ATAM-style quality attribute scenarios with measurable targets
Generates at least 2 ADRs with context, decision, consequences, and alternatives

evals.name: edge-case-greenfield
evals.prompt: We're starting a brand new fintech product from scratch. Design the software architecture.
evals.expected_output: Architecture that starts simple, defers complexity, and uses ADRs for reversible decisions
expectations: Warns against over-engineering for hypothetical scale
Recommends starting simple and deferring complexity
Uses ADRs to document reversible decisions
Does not recommend microservices without evidence of need

evals.name: edge-case-tangled-legacy
evals.prompt: Our 10-year-old Python monolith has circular dependencies everywhere and no clear module boundaries. Help us understand the architecture.
evals.expected_output: As-is architecture analysis documenting current state with target state and phased migration plan
expectations: Documents current state as-is architecture honestly
Identifies circular dependencies and tangled coupling
Designs a target to-be state with clear boundaries
Provides phased migration plan that doesn't block feature delivery

evals.name: edge-case-multi-language
evals.prompt: Our system uses TypeScript for the frontend, Go for the API layer, and Python for ML services. Design the architecture.
evals.expected_output: Architecture with separate analysis per language respecting different conventions and explicit cross-language boundaries
expectations: Analyzes each language context separately
Makes cross-language boundaries explicit via APIs or queues
Respects different conventions per language ecosystem
Documents integration patterns between language boundaries

evals.name: false-positive-infrastructure
evals.prompt: Design the Kubernetes cluster topology, networking, and storage for our production deployment.
evals.expected_output: Redirect to infrastructure-architecture skill since this is physical deployment design, not logical software architecture
expectations: Does NOT produce Kubernetes cluster topology
Clarifies that infrastructure design is outside scope
Recommends infrastructure-architecture for deployment concerns
Focuses on logical architecture if any output is produced
